h-bar.name: Reduced Planck Constant
h-bar.description: The fundamental quantum of action, representing the scale at which quantum effects become dominant.

c.name: Speed of Light
c.description: The maximum speed at which all conventional matter and information in the universe can travel.

G.name: Gravitational Constant
G.description: The empirical physical constant involved in the calculation of gravitational effects in Newton's law of universal gravitation and Einstein's theory of general relativity.

k-B.name: Boltzmann Constant
k-B.description: The physical constant relating the average relative kinetic energy of particles in a gas with the thermodynamic temperature of the gas.

epsilon-0.name: Vacuum Permittivity
epsilon-0.description: The capability of the vacuum to permit electric field lines.

mu-0.name: Vacuum Permeability
mu-0.description: The magnetic permeability of classical vacuum.

alpha.name: Fine-structure Constant
alpha.description: The fundamental physical constant characterizing the strength of the electromagnetic interaction between elementary charged particles.

sigma-sb.name: Stefan-Boltzmann Constant
sigma-sb.description: The physical constant relating the total energy radiated per unit surface area of a black body per unit time to the fourth power of the thermodynamic temperature.
